proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 252
; PROVEN: report.violations >= 1 at line 252.
; The guard "if (report.violations > 0)" establishes this before the log fires.
; The ternary (violations === 1 ? "" : "s") is therefore applied to a positive integer —
; the singular/plural distinction is semantically valid.
(declare-const violations Int)
(declare-const line252_reached Int)
; Code transition: line 252 is inside if (report.violations > 0)
(assert (=> (= line252_reached 1) (> violations 0)))
; We are at line 252:
(assert (= line252_reached 1))
; Negation: can violations = 0, producing "0 violations found" with plural suppressed?
(assert (= violations 0))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: REACHABLE: violations > 0 but exit code 0 is returned when ci = false.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 252
; REACHABLE: violations > 0 but exit code 0 is returned when ci = false.
; process.exit(1) at line 252's enclosing block fires only inside if(ci).
; When ci = false: runVerify returns Promise<void> normally → main() returns →
; the process exits with code 0 (implicit). CI systems cannot detect the failure.
(declare-const ci Int)          ; 1 = --ci flag present
(declare-const violations Int)  ; report.violations from runVerifyOnly
(declare-const exit_code Int)   ; effective process exit code (0 = success, 1 = failure)
; Code transition: runVerifyOnly computes violations unconditionally (before if(ci))
(assert (> violations 0))
; Code transition: process.exit(1) is inside if(ci) { if(violations > 0) { exit(1) } }
; When ci = 0, that block is skipped entirely
(assert (= ci 0))
(assert (=> (= ci 0) (= exit_code 0)))
; Violation: non-zero violations, yet process signals success to the operating system
(assert (= exit_code 0))
(check-sat)

; PRINCIPLE: P5

; PROVEN: report.violations >= 1 at line 252.
; The guard "if (report.violations > 0)" establishes this before the log fires.

(assert (= violations 0))
(check-sat)

; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 252
; REACHABLE: violations > 0 but exit code 0 is returned when ci = false.
; process.exit(1) at line 252's enclosing block fires only inside if(ci).
; When ci = false: runVerify returns Promise<void> normally → main() returns →
; the process exits with code 0 (implicit). CI systems cannot detect the failure.
(declare-const ci Int)          ; 1 = --ci flag present

(declare-const exit_code Int)   ; effective process exit code (0 = success, 1 = failure)
; Code transition: runVerifyOnly computes violations unconditionally (before if(ci))
(assert (> violations 0))
; Code transition: process.exit(1) is inside if(ci) { if(violations > 0) { exit(1) } }
; When ci = 0, that block is skipped entirely
(assert (= ci 0))
(assert (=> (= ci 0) (= exit_code 0)))
; Violation: non-zero violations, yet process signals success to the operating system
(assert (= exit_code 0))
(check-sat)
